cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with an easy walk along the creek trail. It's shaded, flat, and genuinely pleasant — good way to shake off the morning before the day picks up.
cards.why: Cool morning air and a peaceful trail is the best possible start to a Wednesday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Swing through the SCU Project Room to catch Jessica Monette's exhibition. It's intimate, thought-provoking work about land and belonging — the kind of show worth taking your time with.
cards.why: Free contemporary art in a quiet gallery setting is the perfect mid-morning stop.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Triton for a deeper look at the current collection, then wander the sculpture garden out back. It's free, it's gorgeous, and it never feels crowded on a weekday.
cards.why: Two great art stops in one morning without spending a dime.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Get the brisket plate at Smoking Pig and don't skip the mac and cheese. It's casual, no-fuss, and the BBQ here is legitimately the best in the South Bay.
cards.why: You've earned a proper lunch after a morning of walking and gallery time.

cards.name: The Sense USA_ Santa Clara
cards.address: 1841 Pruneridge Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at The Sense and let the afternoon melt away. It's a proper spa experience with top-tier therapists and a calm, clean space.
cards.why: A midweek afternoon massage is one of those things that sounds indulgent until you're actually on the table.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka in Los Gatos. Get a table on the patio if the weather cooperates, order the lamb, and take your time with it. This is the nicest meal you'll have all week.
cards.why: Los Gatos on a Wednesday evening is exactly the right vibe for a relaxed, slightly dressed-up dinner.

cards.name: Student Candlelight Mass
cards.blurb: End the night at the SCU Candlelight Mass. Even if you're not religious, it's a genuinely beautiful way to close out the evening, with candlelight and quiet music in a classic campus chapel.
cards.why: It's free, it's peaceful, and it puts a perfect cap on a full day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Los Gatos Creek Trail — it's flat, shaded, and runs right along the water, so even little kids can handle it without complaint.
cards.why: Cool morning air and a creekside walk is the perfect low-key way to kick things off.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: The Tech Interactive is basically a giant playground for curious kids. Everything is hands-on and the exhibits are genuinely cool for adults too — plan to stay a full two hours because the kids won't want to leave.
cards.why: It's an indoor day and this place was built for exactly this kind of family morning.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ for lunch. Get the brisket plate and the mac and cheese and let the kids go to town — it's casual, it's messy, and it's some of the best BBQ in the South Bay.
cards.why: After a busy museum morning, a big comforting BBQ lunch hits just right.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Kids Knitting at the Los Gatos library is free and open to ages 7 and up. Drop in, grab some yarn, and let the kids try something totally different from screens for an hour.
cards.why: Free, creative, and a great change of pace for the afternoon.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino, browse the shops, and let the kids stretch their legs before dinner. The outdoor space is relaxed and easy to navigate with a family in tow.
cards.why: It's a laid-back spot to decompress and kill some time before dinner.

cards.name: Luna Mexican Kitchen
cards.address: Luna Mexican Kitchen, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Luna Mexican Kitchen in Campbell. Solid tacos, a chill patio, and an easygoing vibe that works perfectly with kids in tow.
cards.why: A low-key, affordable dinner close to home is exactly how you want to end a full family day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail at your own pace — it's shaded, flat, and genuinely pretty. Good for a long easy stroll or a faster-paced walk if you want to move.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window before it gets overcast.

cards.name: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting
cards.address: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting Co.
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a warm cup from this beloved local roaster before heading to the trail — grab a drip coffee or latte and settle in for a few minutes before the day picks up.
cards.why: A quick coffee in your anchor town is the perfect low-key way to ease into a busy Wednesday.

cards.name: Intro to Medicare Workshop
cards.blurb: Free workshop walking through Medicare basics — useful stuff if you're at that stage of life or just want to get ahead of it.
cards.why: It's free and happening today, so why not make it part of the morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through rotating and permanent collections at this free community art museum — the sculpture garden and contemporary galleries make for a relaxed, unhurried afternoon browse.
cards.why: It's a calm indoor option that fits nicely after the workshop and keeps you in Santa Clara without needing to move far.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Pull up a seat at this upscale Greek-inspired spot on Los Gatos's main strip — a late lunch or early pre-spa bite with mezze plates and a cozy dining room is the move on a cool April afternoon.
cards.why: It lands you back near your anchor in Los Gatos and sets you up perfectly before the 5 PM foot spa appointment.

cards.name: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic
cards.address: 2989 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book a foot soak or a quick massage to shake off the afternoon. Good way to reset before dinner.
cards.why: After a full day of walking, this is exactly what you need.

cards.name: CineLux Los Gatos Theatre
cards.address: 43 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Catch a film at this charming neighborhood theater right on North Santa Cruz Avenue — check what's playing and settle into a classic moviegoing experience in a historic downtown setting.
cards.why: After a day of walking and wellness, an evening movie in your own anchor town is the easiest and coziest way to wind down.

cards.name: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting
cards.address: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting Co.
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a proper cup at Los Gatos Coffee Roasting Co. Grab a table outside, let the kids get some hot chocolate, and ease into the day watching the town come alive.
cards.why: Best coffee in Los Gatos and a chill way to start the morning before the activities kick in.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Vasona Lake for a morning walk around the lake. The trail is easy and flat, perfect for little legs, and the kids will love spotting ducks along the water. Check if the Billy Jones Wildcat Railroad is running for a bonus treat.
cards.why: It's the go-to family park in Los Gatos and the lake views are lovely even on a cloudy April morning.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Homework Help K-6 grade
cards.blurb: Are you a student in grades K-6th and need help with homework? Our tutors will provide free homework help on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays from 4:00p.m. - 5:
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Swing back to Los Gatos for Kids Knitting at the library on Main St. Ages 7 and up get to learn the basics in a relaxed, drop-in-style session. It's free and a surprisingly fun wind-down after a big day out.
cards.why: A free, creative activity happening today in Los Gatos that makes for a great low-key end to the afternoon.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: End the day at Santana Row Farmers Market for a relaxed dinner-style stroll. Pick up fresh snacks, grab something hot to eat from one of the vendors, and let the kids wander the outdoor space while you wind down.
cards.why: A lively, easy evening spot where the family can graze and decompress without committing to a sit-down dinner.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning on Castro Street and grab coffee and breakfast from one of the cafes along the strip — it's a laid-back block that wakes up early and rewards it.
cards.why: Best low-key morning kickoff in Mountain View.

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Halo Pottery in Los Altos runs morning studio sessions — get your hands in some clay and make something you'll actually want to keep.
cards.why: A creative morning activity that's way more fun than it sounds.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the Computer History Museum — the exhibits trace computing from punch cards to the iPhone era and it's genuinely wild how fast it all moved. Give yourself the full two hours.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, this one lands.

cards.name: Mayan Kitchen
cards.address: 139 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Mayan Kitchen on Murphy Ave for lunch. It's a compact spot with solid food and a short window, so go while they're still serving.
cards.why: Great local lunch before they close up at 2.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail from the Campbell trailhead. It follows the creek through shaded stretches and is as easy or as long as you want it to be.
cards.why: A nice way to reset after lunch on a mild April afternoon.

cards.name: HealthLand Thai Spa
cards.address: 2937 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book an hour at HealthLand Thai Spa and let someone else do the work for a change. Traditional Thai massage in a calm setting.
cards.why: Perfect mid-afternoon wind-down before dinner.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ. Get the brisket plate and don't skip the mac and cheese. It's unpretentious, generous, and genuinely the best BBQ in the South Bay.
cards.why: You want this brisket in your life tonight.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk around Shoreline Lake. The kids can run the open paths, spot birds along the water, and burn off some energy before the day gets going.
cards.why: Fresh air and wide open space is the perfect way to kick off a family morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours inside the Computer History Museum. The exhibits walk you through the whole wild story of computing, and there's plenty of hands-on stuff that keeps kids genuinely interested.
cards.why: It's right in Mountain View and the kind of place where kids and adults are both actually curious.

cards.name: Cẩm Hưng Sandwich and Coffee
cards.address: 903 Reed Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Pick up banh mi and coffee at this Sunnyvale sandwich shop. The sandwiches are stuffed and affordable, perfect for a quick family lunch without a long wait.
cards.why: Under $15 a head and genuinely good food makes it an easy call for the family.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to the Los Gatos Library for Kids Knitting. Kids ages 7 and up get to learn basic knitting in a chill, guided setting. Free and no supplies needed.
cards.why: It's free, creative, and a totally different kind of activity to mix into the day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water. The path is flat and easy, great for little legs, and the creek views are genuinely peaceful after an afternoon of activities.
cards.why: It's a beautiful, easy stretch that the whole family can enjoy without anyone complaining their feet hurt.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day with dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ. Get the brisket plate, don't skip the mac and cheese, and let the kids go to town on the sides. It's a casual, no-fuss dinner.
cards.why: It's the best BBQ in the South Bay and a total crowd-pleaser for families.

cards.name: Bill's Cafe
cards.address: Bill's Cafe, Willow Glen
cards.blurb: Start the day at Bill's Cafe in Willow Glen — get the eggs benedict or a breakfast burrito and settle into the morning.
cards.why: It's the breakfast institution of the South Bay for a reason.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail from the Campbell trailhead — easy, shaded, and peaceful along the creek.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect time to be outside before the afternoon clouds roll in.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ and get the brisket plate. Don't skip the mac and cheese.
cards.why: Best BBQ in the South Bay, full stop.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara — free contemporary art with a sculpture garden worth wandering.
cards.why: Free, genuinely good, and the indoor galleries are perfect for a cool April afternoon.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Pop into Chromatic Coffee for a well-pulled espresso or a pour-over before dinner. Locally roasted and consistently excellent.
cards.why: If you care about coffee at all, this is the stop.

cards.name: Intro to Medicare Workshop
cards.blurb: Swing by Intro to Medicare Workshop and see what's going on.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Weekly open-air farmers market featuring local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the creek — it's flat, shaded, and totally doable with kids of any age.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect time to get outside before it warms up.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ for a brisket plate and mac and cheese — order at the counter and dig in.
cards.why: The best BBQ in the South Bay and totally kid-approved.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Pick out ceramic pieces at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge and paint them however you want — there's no skill required and kids absolutely love it.
cards.why: A rainy-day-proof activity that keeps everyone entertained for a couple of hours.

cards.name: Xfyd Chess Class for Grades 1-5
cards.blurb: Drop the kids into the Xfyd Chess Class for Grades 1-5 in Milpitas and let them pick up some moves from the instructors.
cards.why: Free, structured, and genuinely fun for younger kids who like games and strategy.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through decades of computing history — from room-sized mainframes to the first personal computers. Hands-on exhibits make it easy to lose track of time exploring how Silicon Valley shaped the modern world.
cards.why: It's a natural wind-down after the chess class and a fitting tribute to the region's tech roots on a cool afternoon.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to Japantown for dinner. Get a bowl of udon at Gombei, then pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo on the way out.
cards.why: One of the most unique neighborhoods in San Jose and a genuinely great dinner destination for families.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Stroll the lively pedestrian boulevard lined with boutiques, wine bars, and restaurants. Great for a relaxed evening window-shop or a nightcap at one of the sidewalk patios.
cards.why: It wraps up the day with an easy, walkable San Jose neighborhood vibe after dinner in Japantown.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kenz Coffee Bar on University Ave — grab a coffee and something to nibble while you wake up and get your bearings on a cool April Wednesday.
cards.why: It's a great spot to ease into the day before things pick up.

cards.name: Laudato Si' Intro Headliner Session: What is this text saying to us today?
cards.blurb: Catch this free headliner session at Santa Clara University digging into Laudato Si' — it's a genuinely interesting conversation about ecology, ethics, and what this landmark text means today.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and perfectly timed for Earth Day.

cards.name: Music@Noon – *New Music for Earth Day
cards.blurb: Stay on campus for Music at Noon — new music commissioned for Earth Day performed live, totally free, a nice way to decompress between sessions.
cards.why: It flows directly from the morning session and keeps you on campus without killing time.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara — the contemporary galleries are genuinely surprising and the sculpture garden out back is worth a slow lap even on a cool day.
cards.why: Free world-class art and you're already in Santa Clara, so this is a natural next move.

cards.name: Euro Market
cards.address: 980 El Camino Real #300, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Euro Market on El Camino Real for imported cheeses, charcuterie, chocolates, and pantry finds you won't see at a regular grocery store — it's the kind of place you wander and leave with more than you planned.
cards.why: A fun low-key stop that makes for a great late-afternoon detour.

cards.name: EcoFashion Show 2026
cards.blurb: Head back to SCU for the EcoFashion Show — designers making a real case that sustainability and style aren't in conflict, and it's free to attend.
cards.why: It caps off the Earth Day theme of the whole day in a genuinely fun way.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Oren's Hummus on University Ave — order the hummus plate, add a side of falafel or shakshuka, and enjoy a satisfying dinner that won't break the bank.
cards.why: It's one of the best dinner values on the Peninsula and a perfect low-key way to close out the day.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the kids will go wide-eyed at the giant mechanical computers, old video games, and robot demos. Let them punch buttons and explore at their own pace.
cards.why: It's genuinely fascinating for kids and adults alike, and the indoor setting is perfect for a cool, drizzly morning.

cards.name: The Future of Humanoid Robots
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: After the main floor, pop into The Future of Humanoid Robots exhibition — it's inside the same museum and the kids will lose their minds over the robot displays.
cards.why: Robots and kids are a guaranteed hit, and it's already included with your admission.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oren's Hummus on University Ave for lunch. Order the hummus plate, grab some pita, and let the kids tear into it — it's fast, fresh, and genuinely one of the best lunch deals on the Peninsula.
cards.why: Affordable, casual, and the food is legitimately great for the whole family.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Main Street Cupertino and walk the outdoor plaza. There are plenty of kid-friendly spots to browse, grab a drink, and just wander around the open-air space steps from Apple Park.
cards.why: It's a relaxed afternoon stretch that keeps the day moving without being intense.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into SWEET55 for Swiss chocolates and a sweet treat — let the kids pick something from the case and grab a little something for yourself too.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key sugar break that kids will talk about the whole drive home.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through Triton Museum of Art's rotating galleries, which mix Bay Area artists with nationally recognized names — a calm, unhurried space good for an early evening wind-down.
cards.why: It's a low-key way to close out an art-and-tech-heavy day with something a little more contemplative.

cards.name: Lan Liu Bowling
cards.address: 525 Hamilton Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a lane at Lan Liu Bowling for a casual round after dinner — a classic, no-fuss spot that keeps the evening going without any pressure.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, knocking down some pins is an easy, fun way to decompress.
